$doc: COMMITTED reference roster for the review council. It is NOT the runtime config: at setup, the installer resolves each provider role to its CURRENT flagship model slug and the transport chosen for this machine, then writes local/seats.<host>.json (gitignored). Model slugs are NEVER committed here — recipes use the {MODEL} placeholder so this file never goes stale. {EFFORT} = efforts.default below the critical tier, efforts.max at critical. {PROMPT_TEXT} = the review prompt content (arg transport); stdin transport omits it and pipes the prompt file.

roster.modelGuidance: The Anthropic OPUS line specifically. Use the `opus` alias (tracks the latest Opus) or a concrete claude-opus-* id. NEVER Fable or Mythos (the Claude-5 / Mythos-class line) — setup must confirm the resolved slug is an Opus id.
roster.recursionDisable: --safe-mode (mechanically disables CLAUDE.md, skills, plugins, hooks, MCP)

asExternalCursor.note: Opus-line model via Cursor when the claude CLI is absent (confirm the Cursor slug is an Opus id with `cursor-agent --list-models`). Isolation = read-only `--mode plan` in the runner-provided empty scratch cwd — weaker than native `--safe-mode`; acceptable fallback.

roster.modelGuidance: Use GPT-5.6 Sol unless OpenAI has released a GPT model with a higher numeric version; only a higher version supersedes Sol. Resolve the exact transport-specific slug at setup and never commit it.
roster.recursionDisable: LEOS_COUNCIL_SEAT sentinel + --ephemeral + --sandbox read-only; normal CODEX_HOME is retained for authentication

asNative.note: native Codex pass pins the setup-resolved OpenAI model: GPT-5.6 Sol unless a higher numeric GPT version has been released

asExternal.note: On any non-Codex host, use GPT-5.6 Sol unless a higher numeric GPT version has been released. Uses the host's normal authentication. --ephemeral prevents session files; the inherited LEOS_COUNCIL_SEAT sentinel mechanically refuses nested Leo councils.

asExternalCursor.note: GPT/OpenAI model via Cursor when the codex CLI is absent: select GPT-5.6 Sol unless a higher numeric GPT version has been released, then confirm the exact Cursor slug with `cursor-agent --list-models`. Isolation = read-only `--mode plan` in the runner-provided empty scratch cwd instead of an isolated CODEX_HOME.

roster.modelGuidance: Latest GLM via OpenRouter for the opencode transport, or the Cursor GLM slug (confirm with `cursor-agent --list-models`) for the cursor transport. Resolve at setup; never commit it.
roster.recursionDisable: opencode --agent plan (read-only); or cursor-agent --mode plan (read-only). Both launch in the runner-provided empty scratch cwd, so repo-local agent config never loads. Neither loads CLAUDE.md-style council mandates.

asExternalCursor.note: GLM via Cursor when opencode is absent — only if `cursor-agent --list-models` lists a GLM slug; {MODEL} is the Cursor slug, not the OpenRouter one.

roster.modelGuidance: Latest Gemini flagship via OpenRouter, or the Cursor Gemini slug (confirm with `cursor-agent --list-models`) for the cursor transport. Native Gemini-CLI plan mode is experimental, so OpenCode+OpenRouter (or Cursor) is the firm route. Resolve at setup; never commit it.
roster.recursionDisable: opencode --agent plan (read-only); or cursor-agent --mode plan (read-only) — both in the runner-provided empty scratch cwd

asExternalCursor.note: Gemini via Cursor when opencode is absent — only if `cursor-agent --list-models` lists a Gemini slug; {MODEL} is the Cursor slug, not the OpenRouter one.

roster.modelGuidance: Latest Grok flagship in Cursor or via OpenRouter. For cursor-agent, confirm the exact slug with `cursor-agent --list-models`. Resolve at setup; never commit it.
roster.recursionDisable: cursor-agent --mode plan (read-only) in the runner-provided empty scratch cwd; or opencode --agent plan via OpenRouter

$runtimeSchema.$doc: Shape of the machine-local local/seats.<host>.json the installer writes and the council skill reads. `native` describes how THIS host runs its own-model read-only review pass; `seats` are the EXTERNAL seats = roster minus this host's own provider, strongest-first, with {MODEL} already resolved to a concrete slug.
$runtimeSchema.adapter: optional seat adapter, allow-listed: claude | codex | opencode | cursor-json | raw (raw is explicit-only — an unknown binary without an adapter is an invalid seat); cursor requires cursor-json after an output-contract smoke test
$runtimeSchema.responsePath: cursor-json external seat only: dotted JSON string field containing reviewer text
$runtimeSchema.cwd: optional per-seat working-directory mode: "scratch" (default — the runner creates an empty private dir under local/council/work/<run>/ and removes it after the seat, so repo-local agent config like .cursor/rules or AGENTS.md never loads into a reviewer; the reviewed repo's absolute path is injected as a prompt header) | "repo" (run inside the reviewed repo — opt-out for transports that cannot read outside their workspace; re-opens repo-local instruction injection)
